finding_results.notes: Differentiated the three revelation scenes into visibly different leverage engines. Scene 1 (Jet/Faye, lines 131-147): Jet interrupts Faye's push and pivots directly to authority — issues a hard operational rule with a concrete threat (pulling the antenna), skipping the denial phase entirely. The scene consequence is an explicit rule with teeth. Scene 2 (Jet/Spike, lines 295-341): Spike forces a concrete operational concession by ordering Jet to kill the Bebop's outgoing maintenance ping, then reveals enough tactical detail ('carrying something under the public signal... old... shouldn't still exist') to show he recognized the signal type without naming it. The leverage shifts when Spike's uncharacteristic specificity reveals his fear. The scene consequence is a concrete action order that changes the ship's operational posture. Scene 3 (Faye/Miriam, lines 451-473): Replaced the denial loop with fragmented grappling — Faye asks about dates, does the math against her own cryo-timeline, and arrives at the destabilizing realization that the dates are impossible. Her 'I wasn't anywhere eleven years ago' and 'Not me' break the denial-insistence pattern into confused engagement with impossible information. The scene consequence is a destabilizing new detail: someone with Faye's face was at the Golden Ox while Faye was frozen. Also fixed a continuity detail (line 345): the receiver reference was updated to match Spike taking the receiver with him when he left the bay.

summary: Addressed the single finding (ch02-dialogue-001) by differentiating three revelation scenes that previously repeated the same denial-counterdenial dialogue ladder. Scene 1 now pivots through authority-assertion (Jet issues a hard rule with an antenna-pull threat). Scene 2 now pivots through forced operational concession (Spike orders the outgoing ping killed, revealing through tactical specificity what he won't say directly). Scene 3 now pivots through fragmented grappling (Faye asks dates, does impossible math against her cryo-timeline, arrives at confusion rather than denial). Each scene ends with a distinct on-page consequence: a rule, an action order, and a destabilizing detail respectively. All dialogue revisions honor contraction levels (high for all characters), preserve spoken roughness and character voice signatures, and avoid polishing messy speech into thesis-lines. No new continuity contradictions introduced — checked ages, timeline, object states, and spatial details against the continuity sheet.
